title: Einstein: His Life and Universe
themes: Scientific genius and its social and personal context
Rebellion against authority as a driver of discovery
Unified field theory as forty-year quest
Pacifism and the atomic bomb dilemma
excerpt: The definitive biography of Albert Einstein, weaving together his scientific genius, rebellious personality, and moral conviction into a single compelling portrait — the first to draw extensively on Einstein's newly released private correspondence.
rationale: Root Book #1 for scientists-and-mathematicians. Not chosen for novelty but as the single most complete and accessible comprehensive Einstein biography in English, covering his scientific work, personal life, political commitments, and moral struggles with a level of documentary thoroughness that sets the standard for the leaf.
verifiedSources: Kirkus Reviews: 'An exemplary biography, at once sympathetic and unsparing'
NYT Book Review (Michiko Kakutani, April 2007): 'Mr. Isaacson had the best available help in explicating the series of revelations Einstein brought forth in 1905'
Physics Today (E. L. Schucking, 2008): 'sympathetic biography… carefully researched with extensive notes'
